reviewNotes: Integer-valued solutions are not a separate node.
Notation definition for the cumulative distribution function of a continuous random variable.
Catalog has no dedicated CDF node; mapped to the broader continuous random variable concept and probability-from-density node.
General expectation notation E(X).
Mapped jointly to discrete and continuous expectation/mean nodes because the catalog lacks a single generic expectation node.
Expectation of a function of a random variable.
No dedicated E[g(X)] node; mapped to the broader discrete expectation node.
General variance notation Var(X).
Catalog has no generic variance node; mapped to the discrete random variable variance node as the closest broader concept.
Probability generating function notation G(t).
Binomial distribution notation B(n, p).
Normal distribution notation N(μ, σ²).
Population mean notation μ.
Mapped to population/sample concepts and the general mean node; no dedicated population-mean node exists.
Population variance notation σ².
Closest catalog node concerns population variance estimates; the underlying parameter concept is not represented exactly.
Population standard deviation notation σ.
Sample mean notation x̄, m.
Unbiased sample variance notation s², σ̂² and formula.
Standard normal probability density function notation ϕ.
Mapped to standardisation and the continuous PDF concept nodes.
Standard normal cumulative distribution function notation Φ.
No dedicated standard-normal-CDF node; mapped to the broader standardisation node.
Population product moment correlation coefficient notation ρ.
Sample product moment correlation coefficient notation r.
